goal: Create dev-loop.sh script that automates the plan-then-execute cycle, and document it in README.md

tasks.name: Create dev-loop.sh
tasks.action: Write an executable shell script at the repo root with `set -euo pipefail`. It must: (1) Accept positional args: MODEL, MAX_ITERATIONS, and optional POLL_INTERVAL (default 5s). Print usage and exit 1 if MODEL or MAX_ITERATIONS are missing. (2) Verify .cluster_tokens.env exists (same guard as query.sh). (3) Loop up to MAX_ITERATIONS. Each iteration: print a timestamped iteration header, call `./query.sh $MODEL` with a multi-sentence prompt instructing the agent to call plan_current to get the current task, do the work described in action/verify/done, run tests via run_tests+get_test_results after any code change, fix any test failures before proceeding, then call plan_complete if done or plan_block with a reason if unresolvably stuck. (4) After each query.sh call, parse the most recent plans/plan-*.json to detect plan status: if the top-level `status` field is `completed`, print a success message and exit 0; if any task has `status` == `blocked`, print the blocked task name/reason and exit 1 with an error message. (5) Sleep POLL_INTERVAL between iterations. (6) After exhausting MAX_ITERATIONS without completion, print a warning and exit 1. The plan JSON parsing must use python3 inline (matching the style of plan.sh). The query prompt passed to query.sh must be quoted carefully to survive shell expansion.
tasks.verify: Run `bash -n dev-loop.sh` (syntax check passes). Run `./dev-loop.sh` with no args and confirm it prints usage. Confirm file has execute bit (`ls -l dev-loop.sh` shows `-rwx`).
tasks.done: dev-loop.sh exists at repo root, is executable, passes `bash -n`, and prints correct usage when invoked with no arguments.

tasks.name: Add dev-loop.sh to README.md operational commands
tasks.action: In the `## Operational Commands` section of README.md, add a line for `./dev-loop.sh <model> <max-iter> [poll-interval]` with a short description such as `# Automated plan-execute loop (runs until complete or blocked)`. Place it directly after the `./query.sh` line so the three workflow scripts are grouped together.
tasks.verify: Run `grep -n 'dev-loop' README.md` and confirm the line appears inside the Operational Commands code block, directly after the query.sh line.
tasks.done: README.md contains a dev-loop.sh entry in the Operational Commands section with correct syntax and description.
